text: MLOps Pipeline for Real User Recommendations
textPreview: MLOps Pipeline for Real User Recommendations

text: A recommendation system built from Kaggle interaction data, manual API events, Kafka streaming, MLflow tracking, DVC versioning, FastAPI serving, Prometheus monitoring, and GitHub CI/CD.
textPreview: A recommendation system built from Kaggle interaction data, manual API events, Kafka streaming, MLflow tracking, DVC versioning, FastAPI serving, Prometheus monitoring, and GitHub

text: Opening line: Real recommendation systems are hard because real user behavior is sparse, skewed, noisy, and constantly changing.
textPreview: Opening line: Real recommendation systems are hard because real user behavior is sparse, skewed, noisy, and constantly changing.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: Build a production-style recommendation pipeline that can learn from historical ecommerce behavior and accept new user events through an API.
textPreview: Build a production-style recommendation pipeline that can learn from historical ecommerce behavior and accept new user events through an API.

text:
Recommend relevant products
for each user from implicit
behavior, not from ratings
or labels.
textPreview: Recommend relevant products | for each user from implicit | behavior, not from ratings | or labels.

text:
The data has no explicit
target column and most users
have very few events.
textPreview: The data has no explicit | target column and most users | have very few events.

text:
Track data, models,
experiments, serving,
streaming, monitoring, and
CI/CD as one pipeline.
textPreview: Track data, models, | experiments, serving, | streaming, monitoring, and | CI/CD as one pipeline.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: Why Kaggle Instead of Synthetic Data
textPreview: Why Kaggle Instead of Synthetic Data

text: Free ecommerce APIs usually expose product catalogs, but not rich user-item interaction histories.
textPreview: Free ecommerce APIs usually expose product catalogs, but not rich user-item interaction histories.

metric accent: Real ecommerce events after import

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: The project combines offline historical data with live manual events from FastAPI.
textPreview: The project combines offline historical data with live manual events from FastAPI.

text:
Historical user events are
imported and normalized into
the project schema.
textPreview: Historical user events are | imported and normalized into | the project schema.

text:
A user can send events like
view, addtocart, or
transaction through /event.
textPreview: A user can send events like | view, addtocart, or | transaction through /event.

text:
Both represent user-item
behavior, so the system can
treat them as implicit
feedback.
textPreview: Both represent user-item | behavior, so the system can | treat them as implicit | feedback.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: Offline training and online serving are connected, but they have different responsibilities.
textPreview: Offline training and online serving are connected, but they have different responsibilities.

text:
Ingestion -> split ->
features -> train ->
evaluate -> compare ->
track.
textPreview: Ingestion -> split -> | features -> train -> | evaluate -> compare -> | track.

text:
FastAPI receives requests,
serves recommendations, and
publishes events to Kafka.
textPreview: FastAPI receives requests, | serves recommendations, and | publishes events to Kafka.

text:
Prometheus scrapes API,
model, and drift metrics
from the /metrics endpoint.
textPreview: Prometheus scrapes API, | model, and drift metrics | from the /metrics endpoint.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: Kaggle events are downloaded, cleaned, and saved in the same schema expected by the old project code.
textPreview: Kaggle events are downloaded, cleaned, and saved in the same schema expected by the old project code.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: The event distribution is highly skewed: many users interact once or twice, while a small number interact heavily.
textPreview: The event distribution is highly skewed: many users interact once or twice, while a small number interact heavily.

text:
Most users have very low
event counts, so profiles
are weak.
textPreview: Most users have very low | event counts, so profiles | are weak.

text:
User-item matrix density is
about 0.0044 percent.
textPreview: User-item matrix density is | about 0.0044 percent.

text:
Top-5 recommendation is
difficult when there are
tens of thousands of items.
textPreview: Top-5 recommendation is | difficult when there are | tens of thousands of items.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: The raw events are filtered and split so users in test have enough history to evaluate recommendations.
textPreview: The raw events are filtered and split so users in test have enough history to evaluate recommendations.

text:
Every event has user id,
item id, event type,
timestamp, and optional
transaction id.
textPreview: Every event has user id, | item id, event type, | timestamp, and optional | transaction id.

text:
Users need at least two
unique items for train/test
holdout.
textPreview: Users need at least two | unique items for train/test | holdout.

text:
Processed train and test
event CSVs are produced by
the DVC split stage.
textPreview: Processed train and test | event CSVs are produced by | the DVC split stage.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: Train-Test Split Without a Target Column
textPreview: Train-Test Split Without a Target Column

text: Recommendation evaluation uses a user-level time holdout instead of a normal supervised split.
textPreview: Recommendation evaluation uses a user-level time holdout instead of a normal supervised split.

role: metric panel: Held out for testing
metric accent: Held out for testing

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: Implicit feedback is converted into weighted user-item interactions and item-level signals.
textPreview: Implicit feedback is converted into weighted user-item interactions and item-level signals.

text:
Rows are users, columns are
items, values are weighted
interactions.
textPreview: Rows are users, columns are | items, values are weighted | interactions.

text:
Popularity, trend, event
rates, and category-like
signals support hybrid
scoring.
textPreview: Popularity, trend, event | rates, and category-like | signals support hybrid | scoring.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: We compared collaborative filtering models that are suitable for implicit user-event data.
textPreview: We compared collaborative filtering models that are suitable for implicit user-event data.

text:
Alternating Least Squares
learns latent user and item
factors from implicit
feedback.
textPreview: Alternating Least Squares | learns latent user and item | factors from implicit | feedback.

text:
Bayesian Personalized
Ranking optimizes item
ranking from positive
interactions.
textPreview: Bayesian Personalized | Ranking optimizes item | ranking from positive | interactions.

text:
Logistic Matrix
Factorization models
interaction probability with
latent factors.
textPreview: Logistic Matrix | Factorization models | interaction probability with | latent factors.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: ALS factorizes the user-item matrix and works well for large sparse implicit-feedback data.
textPreview: ALS factorizes the user-item matrix and works well for large sparse implicit-feedback data.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: BPR learns to rank interacted items above non-interacted items.
textPreview: BPR learns to rank interacted items above non-interacted items.

text:
Optimizes pairwise ranking
instead of rating
prediction.
textPreview: Optimizes pairwise ranking | instead of rating | prediction.

text:
Useful when the main goal is
item order in a
recommendation list.
textPreview: Useful when the main goal is | item order in a | recommendation list.

text:
In this dataset it
underperformed ALS because
interactions are very
sparse.
textPreview: In this dataset it | underperformed ALS because | interactions are very | sparse.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: LMF estimates interaction likelihood using latent factors and a logistic objective.
textPreview: LMF estimates interaction likelihood using latent factors and a logistic objective.

text:
Learns user and item vectors
similar to ALS, but with a
probability-style objective.
textPreview: Learns user and item vectors | similar to ALS, but with a | probability-style objective.

text:
Factors, iterations,
regularization, alpha, and
learning rate affect ranking
quality.
textPreview: Factors, iterations, | regularization, alpha, and | learning rate affect ranking | quality.

text:
Included as a second matrix
factorization baseline
beyond ALS and BPR.
textPreview: Included as a second matrix | factorization baseline | beyond ALS and BPR.

text: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.
textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: The final ranking combines collaborative model scores with item and behavior signals.
textPreview: The final ranking combines collaborative model scores with item and behavior signals.

textPreview: Ecommerce Recommender MLOps: Kaggle data, DVC, MLflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Prometheus, and GitHub Actions.

text: The model recommends top-5 items for each test user and checks whether the held-out item appears.
textPreview: The model recommends top-5 items for each test user and checks whether the held-out item appears.

text:
Out of 5 recommended items,
how many were relevant.
textPreview: Out of 5 recommended items, | how many were relevant.

text:
Out of the user's held-out
relevant items, how many
were recovered.

Whether at least one
held-out item appeared in

text: ALS performed best among the three trained models on top-5 offline evaluation.
textPreview: ALS performed best among the three trained models on top-5 offline evaluation.

text:
precision@5 was around
0.001, much lower than ALS.
textPreview: precision@5 was around | 0.001, much lower than ALS.

text:
precision@5 was around
0.0036, better than BPR but
below ALS.
textPreview: precision@5 was around | 0.0036, better than BPR but | below ALS.

text:
data/raw/kaggle_events.csv
is referenced through a .dvc
file.
textPreview: data/raw/kaggle_events.csv | is referenced through a .dvc | file.

text:
dvc.yaml defines split,
features, train, evaluate,
and compare stages.
textPreview: dvc.yaml defines split, | features, train, evaluate, | and compare stages.

text:
dvc repro reruns only stages
affected by changed inputs
or code.
